an10.219:1.1: “Monks, I don’t speak of the wiping out of intentional acts that have been done & accumulated without (their results) having been experienced, either in the here & now or in a further state hereafter.
an10.219:1.2: Nor do I speak of the act of putting an end to suffering and stress without having experienced (the results of) intentional acts that have been done & accumulated.
an10.219:1.3: “That disciple of the noble ones—thus devoid of covetousness, devoid of ill will, unbewildered, alert, mindful—keeps pervading the first direction [the east] with an awareness imbued with goodwill, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
an10.219:2.1: Thus above, below, & all around, everywhere, in its entirety, he keeps pervading the all-encompassing cosmos with an awareness imbued with goodwill—abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an10.219:3.2: ‘Before, this mind of mine was limited & undeveloped. But now this mind of mine is immeasurable & well developed. And whatever action that was done in a measurable way does not remain there, does not linger there.’
an10.219:4.2: If that youth, from childhood, were to develop the awareness-release through goodwill, would he do any evil action?”
an10.219:5.1: “Not doing any evil action, would he touch suffering?”
an10.219:5.3: for when one does no evil action, from where would he touch suffering?”
an10.219:6.1: “This awareness-release through goodwill should be developed whether one is a woman or a man.
an10.219:6.2: Neither a woman nor a man can go taking this body along.
an10.219:6.3: Death, monks, is but a gap of a thought away.
an10.219:6.4: One (who practices this release of awareness) discerns,
an10.219:6.5: ‘Whatever evil action has been done by this body born of action, that will all be experienced here [in this life].
an10.219:6.6: It will not come to be hereafter.’
an10.219:6.7: Thus developed, the release of awareness through goodwill leads to non-returning for the monk who has gained gnosis here and has penetrated to no higher release.
an10.219:7.1: “That disciple of the noble ones—thus devoid of covetousness, devoid of ill will, unbewildered, alert, mindful—keeps pervading the first direction [the east] with an awareness imbued with compassion.…
an10.219:7.2: “That disciple of the noble ones—thus devoid of covetousness, devoid of ill will, unbewildered, alert, mindful—keeps pervading the first direction [the east] with an awareness imbued with empathetic joy.…
an10.219:7.3: “That disciple of the noble ones—thus devoid of covetousness, devoid of ill will, unbewildered, alert, mindful—keeps pervading the first direction [the east] with an awareness imbued with equanimity, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
an10.219:7.4: Thus above, below, & all around, everywhere, in its entirety, he keeps pervading the all-encompassing cosmos with an awareness imbued with equanimity—abundant, expansive, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an10.219:8.2: ‘Before, this mind of mine was limited & undeveloped. But now this mind of mine is immeasurable & well developed. And whatever action that was done in a measurable way does not remain there, does not linger there.’
an10.219:9.2: If that youth, from childhood, were to develop the awareness-release through equanimity, would he do any evil action?”
an10.219:10.1: “Not doing any evil action, would he touch suffering?”
an10.219:10.3: for when one does no evil action, from where would he touch suffering?”
an10.219:11.1: “This awareness-release through equanimity should be developed whether one is a woman or a man.
an10.219:11.2: Neither a woman nor a man can go taking this body along.
an10.219:11.3: Death, monks, is but a gap of a thought away.
an10.219:11.4: One (who practices this release of awareness) discerns,
an10.219:11.5: ‘Whatever evil action has been done by this body born of action, that will all be experienced here [in this life].
an10.219:11.6: It will not come to be hereafter.’
an10.219:11.7: Thus developed, the awareness-release through equanimity leads to non-returning for the monk who has gained gnosis here and has penetrated to no higher release.”
